evals.prompt: Write a PRFAQ for an AI-powered contract review tool aimed at in-house legal teams at mid-market companies. The problem: legal teams spend 60% of their time on low-complexity contract review that blocks faster deal closure.
evals.expected_output: A prfaq.md artifact committed to the resolved layout path with frontmatter type: prfaq. The press release section includes: a headline naming the product and its customer benefit; a sub-headline with a key detail; a problem statement in the customer's words (not legal-industry jargon); a solution description that names what the product does differently; a call to action; and a leadership quote attributing the strategic rationale to a named role. An explicit adoption hypothesis is derived: the first-success event (the one action that proves first value — e.g., legal team completes first contract review in under 5 minutes) and the repeat-value behavior (what brings the user back). The customer FAQ has 3–5 questions answered honestly, including at least one trade-off. The internal FAQ names the riskiest assumption, the hardest technical challenge, and a success metric that is traceable to the named first-success event. No product spec, backlog item, or API contract appears. The resolved path is surfaced before writing.
assertions: The press release includes all six required elements: headline, sub-headline, problem, solution, call to action, leadership quote
The problem statement is written in the customer's words — no legal-industry jargon or abstract framing
The solution describes what the product does differently — not a generic 'AI-powered' claim
The leadership quote attributes to a role and names the strategic rationale — not a placeholder
An adoption hypothesis is explicitly derived: first-success event (a specific behavioral observable) and repeat-value behavior are both named
The first-success event is behavioral and observable — not 'user signs up' or 'user completes onboarding'
Customer FAQ has 3–5 questions answered honestly, including at least one trade-off
Internal FAQ names the riskiest assumption, the hardest technical challenge, and a success metric
The internal FAQ success metric is traceable to the first-success event named in the adoption hypothesis — not a vanity metric like 'user count'
No product spec, acceptance criteria, API contract, or feature backlog appears
The Amazon PRFAQ format is referenced by name; no verbatim template or format definition appears
The artifact is committed to the resolved path with frontmatter type: prfaq; path is surfaced before writing
